TextENGNew: ...A familiar ceiling.
...My mind was still hazy, but I could tell I was lying in my futon, same as every morning.
I glanced at the clock... Despite going to bed so late, I had woken up at precisely my usual time.
Even with so little sleep... the body really does keep to its schedule.
...I was less amazed than exasperated by the mysteries of the human body...
If I lay back down now, I would never wake up again.@k ...I cracked the whip on myself and crawled out of my futon.
Mom asked me about last night over breakfast.
......Apparently someone had called our house asking about Rika-chan and Satoko, too, so Mom seemed to have a vague sense that something serious was going on.
...So you were out late last night with your friends, looking for them?
............Until about three, I think.@k@vS02/01/130100747. .........Yeah.
...Did you find them?@k@vS02/00/132700028. Rika-chan and Satoko-chan, I mean.
Crunch.
......I chewed my toast without tasting it.@k ...It really had no flavor at all.
Just then, I heard the doorbell chime.
I looked up at the clock and saw it was about five minutes past my usual meeting time with Rena.@k ...That had to be her.
...Good morning.@k@vS02/02/130200282. ...Did you manage to get any sleep? Did you?
...I wonder how many hours I got.@k@vS02/01/130100749. Probably few enough to count on a chicken's toes.
Ahahahaha.
Rena looked sleep-deprived too.@k ...She was missing some of her usual energy.
......Are you... going to school?
Yeah...@k@vS02/02/130200285. ......I think it would be better if we did.
She said it with a smile, stiff as it was.
...Rena had to be just as worried sick about those two.
...And yet she wanted to go to school.

...If you stayed home today, Keiichi-kun, I think you'd just lie there worrying yourself sick.@k@vS02/02/130200287. ...I don't want that to happen.@k@vS02/02/130200288. ...So let's go to school together.
changeReason: New adds vocative 'Keiichi-kun' present in Japanese and better captures 病んで病んで寝込んじゃう (worry yourself sick) vs old's vague 'worrying non-stop and sleeping.'

TextENGNew: ..........All right.@k@vS02/01/130100752. Give me a moment.@k@vS02/01/130100753. I'll be ready in no time.
Rena's thoughtfulness... made me just a little bit happy.
We arrived at the spot where we usually met up with Mion, but she wasn't there.
...We were running a bit late today, so I'd expected her to be waiting for us...
...Maybe Mii-chan just couldn't get up today, huh.
Ah... right.@k@vS02/01/130100755. ...It wasn't just yesterday for her.@k@vS02/01/130100756. ...She was up all night searching for the mayor the night before, too...
We waited a little while.@k ...When it was time to go, we exchanged glances.
What should we do...?@k@vS02/01/130100758. Wait a bit longer?
At that moment, my brain -- finally roused by the morning air -- recalled the ominous words Ooishi-san had left me with last night.
......The heads of the Three Families were vanishing one after another.@k ...Next... it might be the head of the Sonozaki family.
Could Mion have............ No, that was impossible......
......Let's go.@k@vS02/02/130200291. Let's just let her sleep today.
Rena gave me a light pat on the back.
...Mion... she's still here, right?
Hm?@k@vS02/02/130200293. Did you say something?
Oh... no, just talking to myself.@k@vS02/01/130100761. ...Let's go.
The two of us headed to school alone.
Along the way, we passed several classmates walking with their parents.
...It looked like some were even being driven to school by car.
At the school gate, the principal himself was standing guard.@k I'd never seen that before.
Good morning.
Good morning.
The principal was bowing to the parents and guardians.@k ...The wooden sword propped up beside him was a bit unsettling.
Maebara-kun, good morning.@k@vS02/00/133200003. Ryuugu-kun, good morning.
...G-Good morning.
Your teacher has something important to tell you, so head straight to the classroom.@k@vS02/00/133200005. ...Is that clear?
Neither Rena nor I said a word.
......The madness that had only lurked in the night had finally seeped out into the light of day...@k ...That was how it felt.
Good morning, everyone.
The whispered conversations that had been spreading moments ago vanished like a lie the instant our teacher walked in.@k ...A heavy silence took hold of the room.
The principal came in alongside our teacher.@k ...This was clearly no ordinary morning.
...First, the principal has something important to tell you all.@k@vS02/22/131400017. ...Go ahead, Principal.
After clearing his throat, the principal stepped up to the podium.
......Some of you may already know this.
...The classroom was dead silent.
......Everyone's eyes were fixed on Rika-chan and Satoko's empty seats.
The principal stated briefly and simply... that Rika-chan and Satoko had gone missing.
...There wasn't a single person in the classroom who didn't already know.@k ...But there were many, it seemed... who couldn't accept it as reality.
Now, with the principal's words, it had been made official.
......Sniffling and stifled sobs began to rise from all around the classroom...
...They gradually spread through the entire room...
...Even the normally stalwart principal looked like he could barely hold himself together.

All right, everyone, eyes up here!@k@vS02/22/131400019. Starting today, you will go to and from school with a parent or guardian.@k@vS02/22/131400020. Students whose parents can't manage that will travel in groups.
I've prepared a handout, so read it carefully and make sure to show it to your parents.@k@vS02/22/131400022. Understood?
There was an edge to the teacher's voice.
...Even she couldn't hide the strain of this unprecedented crisis...
I thought they should have just cancelled school.
But according to Rena, the school also served as a kind of daycare for students whose parents both worked, so some families wouldn't want it cancelled.
Well then, let's begin class.@k@vS02/22/131400024. ...The class president... is absent today, I see.@k@vS02/22/131400025. Then whoever's on day duty, give us the signal!
All riiiise!
Precisely because our daily lives had gone so mad, the desire to maintain some normalcy within the walls of the school... was something I could agree with, at least for now.
Our teacher, who usually ate lunch in the staff room, had her meal in the classroom today.
......I had to smirk at her curry bento -- the rumors were true after all -- but there was nobody to share the laugh with.
Rena brought her chair and bento box over to my desk.
...Today... there were only the two of us.@k ...The fact that we didn't need to push any desks together... was painful.
...I'm sleepy.
...Yeah.@k@vS02/01/130100763. ...I don't really feel it anymore, though.@k@vS02/01/130100764. ...If anything, I envy anyone who could sleep at a time like this. Nerves of steel.
Ahahaha, maybe so.
I had been sleepy this morning... but after arriving at school and sitting in that taut atmosphere, my drowsiness had completely evaporated.
Rena's bento lacked its usual flair.
...That was only natural.
Rena had been up extremely late last night too.
...She wouldn't have had time to prepare her lunch beforehand.@k And it wasn't as if she'd gotten up early this morning, either...
...Help yourself, no need to hold back.@k@vS02/01/130100766. Just go ahead and admit my bento is better than yours today.
O-Okay... then I won't hold back.@k@vS02/02/130200298. ...Oh, a chikuwa roll with shiso and cheese? That's creative.
Yours might be all store-bought today, Rena, but it's pretty tasty too.
......I could tell we were both desperately trying to keep lunchtime feeling as normal as possible.
...I looked around the classroom... and we seemed to be the only ones making that effort. The gloom around us hadn't lifted at all.
......Ufufu!@k@vS02/02/130200300. Sharing our lunches like this, just the two of us... it's kind of... well...... .........kind of something, isn't it! ...Hau~
Hahaha... haha...
Our laughter... trailed off.@k ...I was the one who faltered first...
Sorry.@k@vS02/01/130100771. ...I mean... I'm sorry.
......No, it's okay.@k@vS02/02/130200302. ...It can't be helped.
Both our chopsticks... stopped.@k ...We couldn't keep up the pretense any longer.
...Rika-chan and Satoko... where could they have gone?
Perhaps those words were too blunt.
...Everyone had been thinking it, tiptoeing around it... and I had just blurted it out.@k I could feel Rena catch her breath...
............Yesterday, while you were away, I heard all kinds of things... so let me tell you what I know.
..........I'm sorry about last night.@k@vS02/01/130100774. ...Go ahead, please.
While I had been gone, Rena had been helping the ladies' association make miso soup.
The villagers had been reporting the information they gathered as it came in, and Rena said she had listened to all of it.
...But in the end, they didn't learn anything useful, did they?@k@vS02/01/130100776. They went home after school and apparently rode their bikes somewhere, but beyond that...
Mion had said the same thing yesterday.@k ...There were no leads whatsoever.
...Yeah.@k@vS02/02/130200305. ...The police said the same thing -- that they probably went out to play on their bikes right after getting home.
...If they went somewhere by bike right after getting home... it might have been into town.
...So the working theory is that they disappeared somewhere in Okinomiya.@k@vS02/02/130200308. The police said they'd expand the search to Okinomiya once it was light out.
In town...
......That would explain why we couldn't find them no matter how thoroughly we searched Hinamizawa.
...But still... something didn't sit right.
......I had no real basis for it... just a gut feeling.
That day, I had confided something to Rika-chan that was likely very important.
...And Rika-chan had told me to leave it to her.@k ...Then... let's see, what did she say again...
Right -- she said that if she didn't do her best, something bad might happen to the "dog" too, or something like that.
Dogs and cats and whatnot... I still had no idea what she truly meant, but... the way Rika-chan spoke, it sounded like she felt every second counted.
...Damn it!!!
Back then, I had just... dumped my fears on Rika-chan and tried to run away!
Thinking about it now... I shouldn't have fled.@k I should have pressed her further... about the dogs and cats.
...The more I looked back on it... what a colossal blunder!!
...At any rate... I didn't think Rika-chan was in any state of mind to casually go out and play.
...Of course, I had no evidence or solid grounds for that.@k Just a hunch.
..........I don't think... she would have gone into town.
You think so too, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS02/02/130200310. Rena thinks so too.@k@vS02/02/130200311. Rika-chan and Satoko-chan must have disappeared right here in Hinamizawa.
I turned to Rena with a start.
...Her words had carried an unmistakable ring of conviction.
Without responding, Rena picked up her bento box and headed for the hallway.
...I'm going to go rinse this out.
The timing was deliberate -- meant to cut the conversation short.
...I instinctively sensed that what she wanted to say next wasn't something she could discuss in the classroom.
I hurriedly grabbed my own bento box and followed her.
...Rena didn't go to the kitchenette. Instead, she headed for the water fountains behind the school, where students rarely went.
...Water splashed as we rinsed out our bento boxes.
...The whole time, Rena didn't say a word.
Only after confirming that no other students were nearby did she finally speak.

...The idea that Rika-chan and Satoko-chan went off to town to play and then vanished...@k@vS02/02/130200313. it just didn't add up for me...... so I did some asking around and investigating on my own.
...Asking around?@k@vS02/01/130100780. About what?

I asked the ladies from the women's association all sorts of things.@k@vS02/02/130200315. ...Remember how there was lots of tofu in the miso soup yesterday?@k@vS02/02/130200316. ...The old lady from the Tomita Tofu shop was there too.
......The Tomita Tofu shop...
...Oh, right.
It was one of the shops along the road to the hospital.
Those big blocks of tofu bobbing around in a bathtub-sized water tank always looked so refreshing.
The old lady remembered Satoko-chan stopping by the shop to buy tofu on her way home from school yesterday.
...Tofu?@k@vS02/01/130100782. It's on the way home, sure, but...@k@vS02/01/130100783. How is that a clue?
Just listen.@k@vS02/02/130200319. ...Do you remember when we went to Rika-chan's house?
Rena stepped away from the water fountains and spread her arms wide, recreating the layout of Rika-chan's room from memory.
......There was a pot on the gas stove, and it had miso soup in it.@k@vS02/02/130200321. I think about half a block of tofu was in there.@k@vS02/02/130200322. The other half was in the refrigerator.
They must have been planning to have hiyayakko -- chilled tofu.@k@vS02/02/130200324. It was on a plate, covered in plastic wrap.
As she explained, Rena paced around as if Rika-chan's room were really right there in front of us.
But Rena... what does any of that matter?
Keiichi-kun.@k@vS02/02/130200326. When you make miso soup, you put the tofu in at the very end.
That means whoever was cooking -- Rika-chan or Satoko-chan -- was standing at that stove right up until dinnertime.
Right up until... dinnertime...?!
Wait... yesterday, the theory was that their bikes were missing, so the two of them must have gone off somewhere to play...?!

The scraps in the kitchen strainer were cut really clumsily.@k@vS02/02/130200329. ...Rika-chan does most of the cooking and bento-making, but occasionally Satoko-chan takes a turn.@k@vS02/02/130200330. So that night, Satoko-chan was the one cooking.
...So that means... Satoko, at least, didn't go out to play... she was preparing dinner...?!
Yes.@k@vS02/02/130200332. Then I checked the rice cooker.@k@vS02/02/130200333. ...There was rice for two still in it.
...So she made dinner for the both of them, but neither actually ate.@k@vS02/01/130100788. ...Is that what you're saying?
Rena nodded.

Then I looked inside the refrigerator.@k@vS02/02/130200335. There was the chilled tofu, made from the other half of the block.@k@vS02/02/130200336. But that wasn't all.@k@vS02/02/130200337. There were several plates of dinner side dishes in there too, and they were all covered in plastic wrap.
......You wrap things in plastic when you want to save leftovers for later.
...My mom does the same thing all the time -- reusing them for breakfast or bento side dishes.

Exactly.@k@vS02/02/130200339. You use plastic wrap to preserve leftovers.@k@vS02/02/130200340. ......The fact that they were in the fridge means they had no intention of eating that evening, at the very least.
......Umm... is that really what it means...?
Keiichi-kun.@k@vS02/02/130200342. Think harder.@k@vS02/02/130200343. ...The dinner was completely untouched and wrapped up.@k@vS02/02/130200344. What do you think that means?
......Umm... well... then they must have lost the need to eat dinner.@k@vS02/01/130100791. ...Like if they ate out, or someone brought food over.
And it had to be sudden.@k@vS02/02/130200346. If they'd planned it ahead of time, they wouldn't have cooked at all.@k@vS02/02/130200347. ...And the dinner was prepared for two.
That means that when Satoko-chan was cooking, she genuinely believed, right up until the very last moment, that the two of them would eat together.
...So, then... the theory that they went out to play and disappeared... is wrong...!!
That's right.@k@vS02/02/130200350. ...They disappeared in the tiny window of time after dinner was ready, but before they sat down to eat.@k@vS02/02/130200351. ...Probably around seven o'clock.
......At that hour, without even eating... where could they have gone...?
That's the obvious next question.@k@vS02/02/130200353. ...Keiichi-kun, do you remember?@k@vS02/02/130200354. Their folding table had soy sauce and a chopstick holder and things like that on it.
...Maybe there was something like that.@k ...I couldn't remember.

The little bottle of soy sauce.@k@vS02/02/130200356. It was empty.@k@vS02/02/130200357. Not a single drop left.@k@vS02/02/130200358. You can't enjoy hiyayakko without soy sauce.@k@vS02/02/130200359. So I looked under the sink for the large bottle they keep there.
...You sure know a lot about where they keep things.
Ahahaha, I've cooked at Rika-chan's place before, so.
Rena cleared her throat and returned to a serious expression.
So when I opened the cabinet under the sink, the large bottle of soy sauce was gone entirely.
...The large bottle was gone... and what does that mean...?
Everything from here on is just Rena's speculation.@k@vS02/02/130200363. ......So please hear me out without interrupting.
...Last night.
Satoko was preparing dinner as usual.
Rika-chan typically watched TV until the food was ready, so she was probably lying down watching a variety show or something.
Satoko added the tofu to the miso soup, and just as dinner was almost ready, she noticed they were out of soy sauce.
So Rika-chan, who had nothing to do, took the large soy sauce bottle and went to a neighbor's house to borrow some.
...I don't really know how the neighbor thing works... but can you actually just ask for soy sauce like that?
Yeah. It's not unusual at all in Hinamizawa.
And so Rika-chan rode her bike out to borrow some soy sauce.
But no matter how long she waited, Rika-chan didn't come back.
So Satoko called the house where Rika-chan had gone.
...Is Rika over at your place, by any chance?@k Something along those lines.
And the person on the other end must have replied with something like this:
'...We have dinner ready, so why don't you come over too, Satoko-chan?@k@vS02/02/130200365. Rika-chan is already eating.'@k@vS02/02/130200366. ...I think that's how they lured her out.
Satoko must have grumbled under her breath as she wrapped up the dinner she'd made.
She put it in the fridge so she could repurpose it for breakfast or bento the next day.
Then Satoko got on her bike too.@k Heading for the house where Rika-chan supposedly was.
But... something is already off at this point.@k@vS02/02/130200368. You don't normally make enough food to serve unexpected guests who show up out of nowhere.
No experienced homemaker would accidentally make enough extra food to feed two more people.
...Couldn't it have just been a coincidence? Maybe they happened to make too much...?
Even so, it's inconceivable.
Rena dismissed the idea outright.

Because... Rika-chan knew that Satoko-chan had worked hard to make dinner for them, right?!@k@vS02/02/130200371. No matter what food she was offered, she would never let Satoko-chan's dinner go to waste.
......All of this was just circumstantial evidence and Rena's conjecture.
...But the persuasive power behind it... was overwhelming.
It was the only ray of light capable of breaking through this situation where we had absolutely no leads.
...Then, Rena.@k@vS02/01/130100801. ...Where did Rika-chan go... to borrow soy sauce?!
That was the crux of it...!!
Someone Rika-chan trusted enough to ask for soy sauce... and someone whose dinner invitation wouldn't seem suspicious to Satoko.@k ......Who was it?!
Rena slowly shook her head...
......That's as far as Rena's reasoning goes.@k@vS02/02/130200373. ...This is all just speculation, remember.@k@vS02/02/130200374. Please keep it from the police.
There's no reason to hide it...@k@vS02/01/130100803. Even if only half of it is right, it should still be a valuable lead...
Keiichi-kun.@k@vS02/02/130200376. ...This means suspecting someone from Hinamizawa.@k@vS02/02/130200377. ...We can't cast suspicion on a villager without real evidence.
I saw a few girls heading our way to rinse out their bento boxes.
...Rena ended the conversation there and went back to the classroom...
I stayed behind alone, letting the lively chorus of cicadas wash over me...
...Let me try to organize my thoughts.
...In my own way.
...Rena had so little information to work with, and yet she'd pieced together all of that.
......Then surely there was something that only I, who knew far more than Rena, could figure out...
Ooishi-san had said it last night too -- this case was unquestionably unfolding within Hinamizawa.
......Rena might not want to believe it, but it was virtually certain that the culprit was someone from the village.
It all started when the four of us trespassed in the forbidden ritual storehouse.
And someone had seen us sneak in.
For the culprits, violating the sanctity of the storehouse was a crime deserving of death.
......And that very night, Tomitake-san and Takano-san were claimed.
That left two.
...Shion and myself.
But before the culprits turned their fangs on Shion and me... they first went after the mayor, to whom Shion had confessed.
And next, they went for Rika-chan, to whom I had confessed...@k ...Then what about Satoko?@k ...Collateral damage...?
Losing Rika-chan, who was targeted because I'd told her everything, was agonizing enough...@k but Satoko, dragged into this for no reason... how much more bitter must that have been...
......It was all... my fault...!!
Who would vanish next?
This time... would it finally be Shion and me...?
...But... why hadn't the culprits already come for us?
If they had the reach to erase the mayor and Rika-chan, then why not just eliminate Shion and me?
If they came after us... well, it wouldn't be pleasant, but I could at least make sense of it.
......What I couldn't forgive was them eliminating the people we'd confided in.
...Come to think of it...@k ...yesterday, Shion had said something unsettling -- that she felt like someone was watching her.
......Then... was someone watching me too...?
Strangely, though... I hadn't felt anything like that until now.
...Despite being the root cause of it all, I had never once had that impression.
Maybe it was simply that Shion was more alert than me, and I was just oblivious.
......I'm getting sidetracked.@k ...Back to the point.
Why hadn't the culprits come after Shion and me?
......I felt like the key to everything lay there.
...Could it be... Was some fundamental assumption of mine wrong...?
.....................No matter how much I thought, no answer came.
The one thing I understood...@k was that I was a party to this case...@k and that I had a duty to see it through until everything was resolved.
The bell signaling the end of lunch break rang in the distance...
Would someone else disappear tonight?
...If someone had to vanish...@k then let it be me tonight.@k ...And let it all end.
changeReason: Old reverses conditional: 'If nobody else did' but Japanese means 'If someone had to vanish.'

TextENGNew: Shion's hysterical voice from last night's phone call echoed in my mind.
...They planned to kill our loved ones first, one by one, making us suffer as much as possible before finally killing us...
changeReason: New adds 'one by one' capturing Japanese 順に (in order) omitted in old.

TextENGNew: Maebara-kun, lunch break is over.@k@vS02/00/133200008. Return to the classroom.
At the principal's urging, I returned to the classroom, quiet as a funeral wake...
changeReason: Old says 'quiet as the streets at night' but お通夜 means 'funeral wake.'

TextENGNew: When school let out, there were many parents waiting to pick up their children.@k ...It looked like a kindergarten.
Students whose parents hadn't come left in groups.
We all walked home together along the designated route.
Nobody exchanged any lighthearted banter on the way.
......They looked like exhausted climbers trudging silently through the mountains.
......One by one, they peeled off, until finally it was just Rena and me.
I was supposed to walk Rena all the way to her house, but she declined.
...Are you sure?@k@vS02/01/130100805. It's not safe to go alone...
You'd be just as unsafe walking home alone after dropping me off, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS02/02/130200379. ...It's still light out, after all.@k@vS02/02/130200380. I'll be fine.
All right.@k@vS02/01/130100807. ...Be careful, then.
Yeah.@k@vS02/02/130200382. ...Huh?@k@vS02/02/130200383. ...Isn't that a police officer?
Rena pointed toward my house.
...There was a car parked out front.
The person who stepped out of it was Ooishi-san.@k ......He must have been lying in wait for my return.
He came walking over with that irritating grin of his.
...Rena, go on ahead.@k@vS02/01/130100809. Once that guy gets hold of you, you'll never get away.
B-But... why would the police want to talk to you, Keiichi-kun? ...Why?
............Rena was a good person.
Even when I had made a fuss about Rika-chan's disappearance, she never asked me why.
...She would have been justified in suspecting me from that moment on.
But even now, she hadn't pressed me.@k ......She didn't rush me into revealing the truth.
......I'm not the culprit.
I know that.
......But... I can't say... that I'm completely uninvolved... in this case.
Rena listened with her usual smile.
...Even after I'd hinted at my connection to the incidents, she didn't look at me with disgust.
...I'll tell you everything, next time.
Okay.@k@vS02/02/130200388. ...I'll be waiting.
...Go on, hurry up.
Rena gave a small bow and trotted off.
...Leaving me behind.@k And Ooishi-san, walking straight toward me.
Well, well.@k@vS02/11/130700214. Welcome home, Maebara-san.
...Hello.
It looks like your family is out.@k@vS02/11/130700216. My, my -- that had me in a bit of a bind.
...He had come all the way to my house...?@k ...I could only be grateful that my parents weren't home.
...If neither of them was here at this hour, they probably wouldn't be back until late.
...I think my dad's out on business.@k@vS02/01/130100815. They probably won't be back until late.
I see.@k@vS02/11/130700218. Well, how convenient.@k@vS02/11/130700219. ...This way we won't give your parents any strange ideas.
Ooishi-san placed a hand on my back and nudged me forward.@k ...It seemed he wanted me to get in his car.
...Are you taking me to the station?
...I wasn't the culprit... but I was the origin of everything.
It wouldn't be strange for him to want to question me.
In that sense, there was a kind of resignation in me...
No, no -- standing around chatting is a bore, so let's just talk in the car.@k@vS02/11/130700221. Come now, after you.
He opened the rear door and gestured for me to get in.
......Once I got in, I wouldn't be getting out easily.
...But if I refused... knowing this man, he might actually slap handcuffs on me and haul me away.
......If I couldn't refuse, I might as well go gracefully.
The car's interior was frigid from the over-cranked air conditioning, and a musty smell wafted from a filter that clearly hadn't been cleaned in ages.
Oh, is it too cold?@k@vS02/11/130700223. Let me kill the engine.
When he turned off the engine, it suddenly went quiet, and the cool, lonesome song of the higurashi cicadas crept inside.
......Kanakanakanakana.
It sounded like a chorus of voices lamenting the far too heavy price that my thoughtless curiosity had brought upon us.
For a while... listening to that chorus, I too mourned the days that had spiraled into madness since that night.
Ooishi-san, as if waiting for me to start talking on my own, lit a cigarette and listened to the higurashi...
The silence grew unbearable... and I was the first to break.
...How is the investigation going?
The investigation?@k@vS02/11/130700225. Which one?@k@vS02/11/130700226. There've been so many lately, I can't keep track. Nfu-fu!
All of them.

...Very well, then.@k@vS02/11/130700228. Officially these are classified investigations under a media blackout, but I'll make an exception for you.@k@vS02/11/130700229. ...Shall we start from the first incident?
...The mysterious deaths of Tomitake-san and Takano-san on the night of Watanagashi.
......On that one, I'm embarrassed to say... we've made no progress whatsoever.

First, Tomitake-san.@k@vS02/11/130700232. There are signs he was assaulted by multiple people, but we can't explain how he clawed out his own throat.@k@vS02/11/130700233. ...We suspected some kind of drug, but nothing was detected.
What about Takano-san...?
Same -- we're completely in the dark.@k@vS02/11/130700235. Autopsies on burn victims are ex-tremely difficult.@k@vS02/11/130700236. We believe she was doused in gasoline after being strangled.

...That case falls under the jurisdiction of the neighboring prefecture's police.@k@vS02/11/130700238. Since they consider it their territory, they haven't been very cooperative.@k@vS02/11/130700239. ...Ah, but that's beside the point.
changeReason: Old reverses territory: says 'in our territory' but Japanese 自分たちの縄張り means 'their territory.'

TextENGNew: ......So the police knew nothing beyond the fact that the deaths were inexplicable.

However, we've placed the estimated time of the crimes at shortly after the festival ended.@k@vS02/11/130700241. ...Takano-san's case is a bit murky, but the most likely scenario is that she was murdered around the same time as Tomitake-san and then dumped in the mountains.
...Why would they carry a body all the way out to the mountains?@k@vS02/01/130100821. ...Why not carry Tomitake-san's body out there too...?

Oh my... Maebara-san, you're surprisingly sharp!@k@vS02/11/130700243. I agree that's a point worth examining.@k@vS02/11/130700244. ...There are several other suspicious details as well... but this is about all I can share at this stage.
...What about the mayor?@k@vS02/01/130100823. Any leads there?
Afraid that once the topic ran dry, it would be my turn on the hot seat, I steered toward the next subject...
We traced his movements on the day he disappeared.@k@vS02/11/130700246. ...We didn't find anything that could serve as a clue, but I'll tell you about it anyway.
The mayor -- Kiichirou Kimiyoshi.
...Apparently, he had been suffering from severe hemorrhoids.
People of his generation have a great deal of pride, you know.@k@vS02/11/130700248. ...So the mayor had been secretly visiting the proctology department at a university hospital in Shishibone City.
On the day he disappeared, he had an appointment, so he left the house first thing in the morning.
Even I knew that big hospitals like university hospitals had notoriously long wait times.
...Apparently, it was common to wait for hours even with an appointment.

His examination ended around one in the afternoon.@k@vS02/11/130700251. After that, he had a light meal in the hospital's restaurant and killed time reading a magazine.@k@vS02/11/130700252. ...We know this from a receipt found in his wallet at home.
......Nothing suspicious about any of that.
That day, he had a meeting scheduled at the shrine's assembly hall in the evening.

...He was supposed to get home with plenty of time to spare, but there was an accident on the train on his way back.@k@vS02/11/130700255. It seems it delayed him quite a bit, and he only arrived home right before the meeting was set to begin.
His family witnessed him rush out in a great hurry, heading for the assembly hall at the shrine.
...What was the meeting about?@k@vS02/01/130100824_1. He disappeared on his way home from it, right?

The Three Families and the key members of the town council were all present.@k@vS02/11/130700259. We believe they were discussing how to handle the serial mysterious deaths that had occurred again this year.
...And then... on his way home from that meeting......
The old man who had been so kind to Shion.
changeReason: Old adds 'like his own daughter' not in Japanese 可愛がってくれた (was affectionate toward); new 'been so kind to' is more accurate.

TextENGNew: ...He must have been such a reassuring presence, encouraging Shion when she was on the verge of being crushed by anxiety.
......Shion's anguished voice pierced my heart...
What's the matter?
I was startled by the odd sound of my own voice.@k ...Just now, what had... surprised me...?
I pressed both hands against my head, trying to drag back out the thought that had flashed through my mind for a split second.
......Remember, Keiichi Maebara!!
You just noticed something!!@k ...Something crucial...!!!
The night of Watanagashi.
That was where it all began.
...That night, Takano-san and the others died.
......By the next morning, word of the incident had spread throughout Hinamizawa.
They arranged to discuss it at the evening meeting.
But the mayor had an appointment at the hospital, so as planned, he went there -- without telling his family.
He had his examination,@k@|@y ate a meal,@k@|@y and got caught in an unexpected delay on the train home.
...By the time he got back, the meeting was about to begin.
The mayor changed his jacket, jumped on his bicycle, and rushed to the assembly hall at the shrine as fast as he could...
From the moment he heard about the Watanagashi night incident... to when he appeared at the assembly hall.@k ......Everything about his day had been a secret.
During that time, unknown to anyone:@k@|@y he was at the hospital alone,@k@|@y he ate alone,@k@|@y he rode the train alone.
......That meant no one could have contacted him from outside.
He couldn't even have been reached by phone.
......But so what, Keiichi Maebara...?@k@rWhat does that matter, Keiichi Maebara...?
............Kei-chan... What should... I do......
...What's wrong?@k@vS02/01/130100594. ...Tell me.@k@vS02/01/130100595. We're not keeping secrets from each other, right...?
I............ I told old man Kimiyoshi... everything.
I told him everything.@k Old man Kimiyoshi.
...I told the mayor... that night... that we snuck into the ritual storehouse.@k@vS02/06/130600463. ...That someone saw us... and someone was after us.
...Old man Kimiyoshi... didn't get angry.

......He smiled... and told me that if I was truly sorry, there was no way I'd be spirited away...@k@vS02/06/130600466. ...He was really... smiling when he said it... Leave it to me... he said............ ...Ngh...!!
Beads of sweat were breaking out across my entire body.
......They merged into a single rivulet... tracing a line from my forehead, down the bridge of my nose... and dripping off.
......So then... what does it mean...?
Hey, Keiichi Maebara... enough playing dumb.........
When did she tell him?
Shion said she'd learned of the incident from a phone call her father had made that morning.
By that time, the mayor had already left the house first thing for his hospital appointment.
He hadn't told anyone where he was going.
......Nobody knew he had gone to the university hospital.
And his train home was delayed by an accident. The moment he returned, he rushed straight out to the assembly hall.
The meeting at the assembly hall began... and it went on until dark.
...Then it adjourned.
...After the meeting, there were no further sightings of him.@k ...He simply vanished.
Which meant... which meant... which meant... ............umm......
Maebara-san?@k@vS02/11/130700262. Are you all right?@k@vS02/11/130700263. If you're too hot after all, I can turn the air conditioner back on.
Another part of me was scrambling my thoughts, demanding that I stop thinking any further.
My head was shaking, convulsing... the contents churning and blending into an incomprehensible mess, as if put through a blender.
Just as it was about to settle, it was smashed to pieces in an instant, losing all form...
...Maebara-san?@k@vS02/11/130700265. Hello?
Ooishi-san lit yet another cigarette.
...The ashtray was already packed with butts.
......Was it a measure of how much time had passed?
...Had I... been frozen in thought for that long...?
I've told you everything I know.@k@vS02/11/130700267. ...In return, I'd like to hear what you have to say, Maebara-san.
My mind had been reduced to... a churning, formless slurry.
...What had once been my brain felt completely ground down by the blades of a blender...... and I couldn't form a single coherent thought.
Now, now, don't go quiet on me again.@k@vS02/11/130700269. ...If there are any misunderstandings, I'd rather clear them up now.@k@vS02/11/130700270. ...I don't think you're the culprit, you know.
Ooishi-san tried his hardest to produce a reassuring smile.
...I know the four of you snuck into the ritual storehouse that night.@k@vS02/11/130700272. By the next day, it was already the talk of the village among those in the know.

...That in itself warrants charges of trespassing, but that's not the issue.@k@vS02/11/130700274. ......The issue is what was inside.@k@vS02/11/130700275. ...What was in there that caused the people who broke in to be sacrificed one after another?
............There was... nothing.
The moment the words left my mouth, I regretted confirming that I'd been inside... but it was too late to matter.
...Was there really nothing?

Because the people who went inside are being picked off one by one, aren't they?@k@vS02/11/130700278. Even the mayor, who changed the lock on the door, and Rika-san -- they've been sacrificed.@k@vS02/11/130700279. ...What was inside that building that could trigger all of this?
I was beyond exhausted from keeping silent...
...This was probably exactly what Ooishi-san was angling for... but I no longer cared.
......There were just... old torture devices, rusted and covered in dust.@k@vS02/01/130100838. ...I looked around at them with the others. That was all.
Was that really all?
...That was really all.
There wasn't something so incriminating hidden in there that someone would silence you over it?
There weren't any suspicious wooden crates or containers piled up in there?
There was nothing... as dramatic as what Ooishi-san was hoping for.
......In a sense, though, I suppose there were terrible enough things hidden inside.
Besides, from the perspective of someone who held the storehouse sacred... it didn't matter whether or not I personally thought what I'd seen was significant.
......The four of us broke the taboo.@k Punish them.@k .........That's how it would go.
changeReason: Old says 'Punish us' but contextually the village perspective demands 'Punish them' (the transgressors); new correctly captures the external judgment perspective.

TextENGNew: ...We tried to request a search warrant, thinking there might be some secret hidden in that storehouse.@k@vS02/11/130700287. ......But the obstruction we ran into was absolutely unprecedented.

They called it an insult to a religious institution, cited constitutional freedom of religion, and so on and so forth...@k@vS02/11/130700289. Good grief!@k@vS02/11/130700290. ...That's why my only option was to ask you directly, since you've actually been inside.
......There really were just... old, rusted, dust-covered torture devices.@k@vS02/01/130100841. ......Nothing else stood out at all.
...Hmm.@k@vS02/11/130700292. ......So Tomitake-san and Takano-san were killed purely as punishment for having broken the taboo?
......"Purely as punishment" -- I had no way to answer that.
...I'll stop beating around the bush.@k@vS02/11/130700294. ...You're the only one who's escaped harm so far.
Everyone but you has vanished, and yet you alone remain unscathed.
...What makes you different from them? Why are you safe while they were targeted?@k@vS02/11/130700297. That's what I want to know.
...Something about the way Ooishi-san phrased that felt off.
...You alone,@k you alone.@k ...What...?
Wait... wait a moment.@k@vS02/01/130100843. What do you mean by 'you alone'?
Na-ha-ha-ha. ...I mean that you alone are the last one standing.
Out of the four thieves who crept into the ritual storehouse.
......The last one standing?
...Hold on. ...Shion was still...

Jirou Tomitake-san and Miyo Takano-san died that night, and Shion Sonozaki-san disappeared the very next day.@k@vS02/11/130700301. ...You are the only one still safe and sound.@k@vS02/11/130700302. ...Why only you?@k@vS02/11/130700303. That is what I want to know.
Sh-Shion disappeared?!@k@vS02/01/130100845. That's impossible!!!
My voice rose involuntarily.@k ...But I didn't care.
Tomitake-san died.@k Takano-san died.@k@|@y ...And Shion vanished?!
When did she disappear...?!?!
What was this man talking about?!?!
...Oh, well now... I assumed you already knew.
...Th-This is the first I've heard of it!@k@vS02/01/130100847. Shion disappeared?! When?!?
Well, tracing it back, she disappeared the day after Watanagashi.
...Tracing it back?!@k What kind of way was that to put it...!!

Shion Sonozaki-san would sleep over at various relatives' houses depending on her mood, so no one keeping tabs on her location was unusual.@k@vS02/11/130700307. She often skipped school on a whim, too.@k@vS02/11/130700308. Her teacher was at a total loss.
I didn't ask about that!!@k@vS02/01/130100849. I'm asking when Shion disappeared!!
I told you -- the day after Watanagashi.
...Every fluid in my body reversed course...
...My sense of balance vanished... and everything in my vision began to warp and distort.
......I couldn't hold myself together...
Wait -- wait.
......Shion had already disappeared...?
Then, then...@k@s0.@a0.Every night... those phone calls... who was calling me...?
changeReason: Old says 'That night, that call' (singular) but Japanese 毎晩 means 'every night' (recurring).

TextENGNew: She called me every night.
She encouraged me.
...We swore to survive together -- swore we wouldn't let them erase us.
......Yes, those were phone calls from Shion -- they were from Shion -- they had to be from Shion...

She skipped school without a care, and even when the school notified her family, they didn't bother following up, so the school stopped calling.@k@vS02/11/130700311. ...As a result, it took quite some time before anyone realized she had disappeared.
A stabbing pain, like needles being driven into every pore of my body.
......It started from my back... and spread across my entire body.
...Once it had covered every inch of my skin... it began worming its way into my stomach as well......
Don't you remember?@k@vS02/11/130700313. The day after Watanagashi, we met at the library.
Shion-san was there too.@k@vS02/11/130700315. ...That was the last confirmed sighting.@k@vS02/11/130700316. No one has verified her whereabouts since then.
That day, Ooishi-san had cornered only me... and Shion had slipped away, using her part-time job as an excuse.
......And then she called me that night, didn't she...?!

Her part-time job was at a family restaurant called Angel Mort, right?@k@vS02/11/130700318. She hasn't shown up since that day.@k@vS02/11/130700319. ...She's been absent without notice every single shift since.@k@vS02/11/130700320. The manager couldn't reach her and was beside himself.

It's only circumstantial evidence, but...@k@vS02/11/130700322. After she left us at the library, and before she could reach her workplace...@k@vS02/11/130700323. ...it's reasonable to conclude she disappeared during that interval.
......Right after Shion... left me behind and walked away...?!

It seems you didn't know.@k@vS02/11/130700325. ...If I'd told you sooner, this might have gone faster.@k@vS02/11/130700326. Surely you understand now.@k@vS02/11/130700327. ...You're the only one left.@k@vS02/11/130700328. You are the sole survivor of the four who snuck into the ritual storehouse.
......It couldn't be...@k .........It just couldn't be...
If something happens next, it will happen to you.@k@vS02/11/130700330. Please understand that, Maebara-san.
...It can't be,@k it can't be,@k it can't be...
...Shion had already -- already disappeared...?!?!
That was impossible. It couldn't be.
...I had talked to Shion the day before yesterday.
I had talked to her last night.
And she was supposed to call again tonight.
That was Shion on the phone -- it was Shion.@k ...It was from Shion -- from Shion.
Shion-san disappeared long ago.@k@vS02/11/130700332. ...You're next.
Then... who had been calling me every night?
Who had been calling me every night?
Who had been calling me every night...?!!
That's... That can't beeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee!!!
